seoTitle: How to Send Your First Email Campaign in Brevo
metaDescription: Launch your first Brevo email campaign from setup to send, covering sender details, subject lines, content design, targeting, and pre-launch testing.
intro: Sending a first marketing campaign can feel intimidating with delivery settings, subject line pressure, and the risk of landing in spam. Brevo's workspace walks you through the process with a drag-and-drop design canvas, smart list filters, and automated scheduling. This guide covers the full path from setup to send.

steps.heading: Set up sender details
steps.body: Assign a recognized sender name, reply-to handle, and optimized preview snippet so recipients know who the email is from before opening it.

steps.heading: Craft a high-open subject line
steps.body: Write a subject line designed to encourage opens without triggering spam filters.

steps.heading: Create visual content
steps.body: Pull in media nodes, stylized headings, and call-to-action buttons using the drag-and-drop content builder.

steps.heading: Pick target subscriber segments
steps.body: Choose which contact lists or segments should receive the campaign.

steps.heading: Run pre-launch tests and commit
steps.body: Send a test preview to check rendering, then commit your final broadcast delivery once everything looks correct.

faq.question: What do I need before sending my first campaign?
faq.answer: You'll need a verified sender identity, a contact list to target, and a completed email design before you can send.

faq.question: How do I avoid landing in spam on my first send?
faq.answer: Using a verified sending domain, writing a clear subject line, and avoiding spammy formatting all help keep your first campaign out of spam folders.

faq.question: Can I schedule a campaign instead of sending immediately?
faq.answer: Yes, Brevo's automated scheduling engine lets you set a future delivery time rather than sending right away.

faq.question: Should I test my first campaign before sending it live?
faq.answer: Yes, running a pre-launch inbox test helps you catch layout or content errors before the real send goes out.
